edges.description: Impaired pituitary development reduces the differentiated hormone-producing cell populations that normally maintain multiple anterior pituitary axes.
Developmental-gene disruption may also produce visible pituitary MRI malformations, including anterior pituitary hypoplasia and ectopic posterior pituitary findings.

nodes.description: ACTH deficiency or central adrenal insufficiency can occur and is clinically important because missed adrenal insufficiency can lead to hypoglycemia or adrenal crisis during illness.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term follow-up data of patients with Multiple Pituitary Hormone Deficiency
evidence.snippet: At admission, GH deficiency was found in 88.9% of the cases, TSH deficiency in 77.8%, ACTH deficiency in 33.3%, FSH/LH deficiency in 22.2%, and PRL deficiency in 17.8%.

nodes.description: Reduced function of multiple anterior pituitary cell lineages causes the core endocrine phenotype: GH deficiency, central hypothyroidism, ACTH deficiency or central adrenal insufficiency, hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and prolactin deficiency in variable combinations.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term follow-up data of patients with Multiple Pituitary Hormone Deficiency
evidence.snippet: At admission, GH deficiency was found in 88.9% of the cases, TSH deficiency in 77.8%, ACTH deficiency in 33.3%, FSH/LH deficiency in 22.2%, and PRL deficiency in 17.8%.

evidence.reference_title: Etiology of combined pituitary hormone deficiency: GNAO1 as a novel candidate gene
evidence.snippet: Of them, 30 had central adrenal insufficiency, 27 had central hypothyroidism, ten had hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and three had central diabetes insipidus.

nodes.description: Posterior pituitary or hypothalamic involvement can produce central diabetes insipidus in a minority of congenital CPHD cases.

evidence.reference_title: Etiology of combined pituitary hormone deficiency: GNAO1 as a novel candidate gene
evidence.snippet: Of them, 30 had central adrenal insufficiency, 27 had central hypothyroidism, ten had hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and three had central diabetes insipidus.

nodes.description: Thyrotroph or central TSH-axis failure can cause low thyroid hormone production as one component of combined pituitary hormone deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Etiology of combined pituitary hormone deficiency: GNAO1 as a novel candidate gene
evidence.snippet: Of them, 30 had central adrenal insufficiency, 27 had central hypothyroidism, ten had hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and three had central diabetes insipidus.

nodes.description: Desmopressin, a vasopressin analog, is used when posterior pituitary or hypothalamic involvement produces central diabetes insipidus.

evidence.reference_title: Etiology of combined pituitary hormone deficiency: GNAO1 as a novel candidate gene
evidence.snippet: Of them, 30 had central adrenal insufficiency, 27 had central hypothyroidism, ten had hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and three had central diabetes insipidus.

nodes.description: Germline variants in pituitary-development, midline-patterning, and lineage-specification genes disrupt pituitary morphogenesis or endocrine cell differentiation. This upstream genetic heterogeneity explains why affected individuals can have isolated anterior pituitary cell-lineage failure, structural pituitary malformations, or broader extrapituitary phenotypes.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic features of childhood-onset congenital combined pituitary hormone deficiency: a retrospective, single-center cohort study
evidence.snippet: Pathogenic variants in POU1F1, GLI2, HESX1, TBC1D32, and ROBO1 were found in 5 (19.2%).

evidence.reference_title: Knockout mice with pituitary malformations help identify human cases of hypopituitarism
evidence.snippet: Of the 209 knockout mouse lines, we identified 51 that have embryonic pituitary malformations.

nodes.description: Recombinant human growth hormone is used for GH deficiency to reduce hypoglycemia risk in early disease and improve growth outcomes.

evidence.reference_title: Management of Neonatal Isolated and Combined Growth Hormone Deficiency: Current Status
evidence.snippet: Early GH replacement therapy leads to more positive outcomes, including reduced hypoglycaemia, growth recovery, metabolic asset, and neurodevelopmental improvements.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of POU1F1 Variants in Vietnamese Patients with Combined Pituitary Hormone Deficiency
evidence.snippet: All patients received levothyroxine and recombinant human growth hormone (rhGH) replacement therapy, leading to considerable growth.

nodes.description: Hydrocortisone or cortisol glucocorticoid replacement is used when ACTH deficiency or central adrenal insufficiency is present, reducing the risk from untreated adrenal-axis failure during illness or stress.

evidence.reference_title: Etiology of combined pituitary hormone deficiency: GNAO1 as a novel candidate gene
evidence.snippet: Of them, 30 had central adrenal insufficiency, 27 had central hypothyroidism, ten had hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, and three had central diabetes insipidus.

evidence.reference_title: An Update on Advances in Hypopituitarism: Etiology, Diagnosis, and Current Management
evidence.snippet: Hormone replacement therapy is the mainstay of treatment, significantly improving patients’ quality of life.

evidence.reference_title: A Novel Missense Variant in LHX4 in Three Children with Multiple Pituitary Hormone Deficiency Belonging to Two Unrelated Families and Contribution of Additional GLI2 and IGFR1 Variant
evidence.snippet: This study confirms that heterozygous pathogenic variants of LHX4 can cause MPHD associated with a specific neuroradiological triad of abnormalities despite incomplete penetrance and variable phenotype.

nodes.description: Levothyroxine is used when central hypothyroidism is present; thyroid-axis replacement should be coordinated with adrenal-axis assessment.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of POU1F1 Variants in Vietnamese Patients with Combined Pituitary Hormone Deficiency
evidence.snippet: All patients received levothyroxine and recombinant human growth hormone (rhGH) replacement therapy, leading to considerable growth.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of POU1F1 Variants in Vietnamese Patients with Combined Pituitary Hormone Deficiency
evidence.snippet: Variants in the POU1F1 gene are associated with combined pituitary hormone deficiency 1 (CPHD1), which manifests as deficiencies in growth hormone (GH), thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH), and prolactin (PRL).

evidence.reference_title: Identification of POU1F1 Variants in Vietnamese Patients with Combined Pituitary Hormone Deficiency
evidence.snippet: All patients were definitively diagnosed with CPHD1 caused by POU1F1 variants.

evidence.reference_title: Comparison of clinical characteristics of a pediatric cohort with combined pituitary hormone deficiency caused by mutation of the PROP1 gene or of other origins
evidence.snippet:
The most commonly identified genetic cause of combined pituitary hormone deficiency (CPHD) is
                      PROP1
                      gene mutations.

evidence.reference_title: Etiology of combined pituitary hormone deficiency: GNAO1 as a novel candidate gene
evidence.snippet: The remaining 6 children had causative genetic variants in the GLI2, PROP1, POU1F1, TBX3, PMM2, and GNAO1 genes, respectively.

evidence.reference_title: Comparison of clinical characteristics of a pediatric cohort with combined pituitary hormone deficiency caused by mutation of the PROP1 gene or of other origins
evidence.snippet: significantly lower prolactin concentrations (128 vs. 416.3

evidence.reference_title: Comparison of clinical characteristics of a pediatric cohort with combined pituitary hormone deficiency caused by mutation of the PROP1 gene or of other origins
evidence.snippet: Secondary adrenal insufficiency was less frequent in CPHD-PROP1 (20 vs. 25 cases,

evidence.reference_title: Comparison of clinical characteristics of a pediatric cohort with combined pituitary hormone deficiency caused by mutation of the PROP1 gene or of other origins
evidence.snippet: MRI of the pituitary gland in CPHD-PROP1 revealed a small pituitary gland (21 cases), pituitary gland enlargement (eight cases), and one pituitary stalk interruption and posterior lobe ectopy, while it was normal in nine cases.

nodes.description: Treatment is tailored to the deficient axes and generally consists of replacement of missing pituitary target hormones, with attention to interactions between replacement therapies.

evidence.reference_title: An Update on Advances in Hypopituitarism: Etiology, Diagnosis, and Current Management
evidence.snippet: Hormone replacement therapy is the mainstay of treatment, significantly improving patients’ quality of life.

nodes.description: GH deficiency is usually prominent and may be the first recognized endocrine deficit, causing growth failure or short stature.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic features of childhood-onset congenital combined pituitary hormone deficiency: a retrospective, single-center cohort study
evidence.snippet: Growth hormone deficiency (GHD) was prevalent in 42 (97.7%), and 33 (76.7%) had 3 or more hormone deficiencies.

nodes.description: Many genetic CPHD cases have pituitary MRI abnormalities, including anterior pituitary hypoplasia, ectopic posterior pituitary, or other midline structural defects that correlate with the number of hormone deficiencies.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic features of childhood-onset congenital combined pituitary hormone deficiency: a retrospective, single-center cohort study
evidence.snippet: Brain MRI abnormalities correlated with a higher number of hormone deficiencies (P for trend 0.049) and were present in 33 patients (80.5%).
